summary: Deliver steers a live instruction into a RUNNING turn (pinned open by a sleep tool): the model obeys the mid-turn word in the same turn's reply
review_criteria: proper formatting — clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable — reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

output.steer_reached_model: steered word only appears if the mid-turn live input reached the model

agent_review.notes: STEER-INTO-RUNNING-TURN proven end to end via the library entry point (opt-in RUN_MCPAGENT_STEER_E2E; live-model mid-turn timing is intermittently flaky — an observed run had claude hang after the steer and never complete the turn, so this is NOT an always-on gate; the routing decision is covered deterministically by TestDecideDelivery and the send-keys mechanism is certified upstream via CertLiveInput/CertBusyLiveInput). On a passing run: the turn was pinned open by a real `sleep 25 && echo COMMAND_DONE` bridge tool call; while it was provably in flight (first ToolCallStartEvent observed, TurnInFlight()==true), Deliver routed to live input (delivery_mode="steered", i.e. decideDelivery(busy,steerable)->steer -> DeliverUserMessage -> send-keys). The final reply carried BOTH the tool's output (tool_completed=true) AND the mid-turn steered word (steered_word) on its own line (steer_word_obeyed=true). The steered word was introduced only after the turn began and could reach the model solely via live input, so its presence isolates true mid-turn steering. Clean, coherent, correctly ordered.
